briefing.title: Sell-Through Rate vs. Competition
briefing.detail: Purse Maison sold 45 units MTD (₱14.07M) on 1,589 inventory — a 2.8% sell-through rate — lagging Miss Manila Luxe (83 units, 17.3% STR at avg ticket ₱77,574) and The Orange Box (21 units, 3.0% STR but ₱35.9M value sold vs. our ₱14.1M on 3x their inventory).
briefing.action: Prioritize velocity on mid-tier Hermès and Chanel units; the volume gap vs. Miss Manila Luxe signals demand exists at lower price points where we are underrepresented.

briefing.title: Hermès Hero Model Pricing Premium
briefing.detail: Our Birkin 25 lowest ask is ₱1,288,000 vs. market lowest at ₱950,000 (+35.6%); Kelly 28 lowest ask is ₱735,000 vs. market lowest ₱480,000 (+53.1%) with 28 market listings competing.
briefing.action: Review the 11 Kelly 28s and 6 Birkin 25s — authenticate condition/hardware tiers and consider repricing at least 2–3 entry-level pieces to within 10–15% of market floor to capture active buyers.

briefing.title: Mini Kelly & Constance 18 Competitive Position
briefing.detail: Mini Kelly (Kelly 20) lowest ask at ₱1,540,000 exceeds market high of ₱1,680,000 by only ₱140,000 with just 4 market listings — thin market; Constance 18 lowest ask ₱404,000 matches market floor exactly with 17 listings competing.
briefing.action: Hold Mini Kelly pricing (scarcity supports it); for Constance 18, ensure our 10 listings are differentiated by condition/color/HW details to avoid being commoditized at the floor price.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo Overstock Risk
briefing.detail: We carry 23 Chanel Double Flap Jumbos (largest single-model count) with lowest ask at ₱188,000 matching market floor, while 35 total market listings create heavy supply pressure at the bottom.
briefing.action: Immediately audit the 23 Jumbos by condition grade — merchandise Grade A pieces at ₱280,000–₱320,000 with styling content, and mark down Grade B/C pieces aggressively to clear within 14 days.

briefing.title: The Orange Box Value Threat
briefing.detail: The Orange Box generated ₱35.9M sold value on only 21 inferred transactions (avg ₱1.71M/unit) vs. our ₱312,600 avg sold ticket — they are winning the ultra-premium Hermès exotic/rare segment we are not capturing.
briefing.action: Source 2–3 Hermès exotics or rare HW combinations for August consignment; even 1–2 units at ₱1.5M+ would materially lift our sold value per transaction.

briefing.title: Inventory Depth vs. Days-to-Sell
briefing.detail: At 11.1 avg days-to-sell on 1,589 units, implied full sellout is ~393 days — we are sitting on ₱285.6M tied up; top velocity peers (Miss Manila Luxe 5.8 days, A&J 6.1 days) carry far leaner stocks.
briefing.action: Set a 90-day aging watch now (currently zero aging90 — good) and flag any unit unsold past 30 days for a 5–8% price reduction cadence before it becomes a 90-day problem.

alerts: The Orange Box avg sold ticket of ₱1,708,905 is 5.5x ours (₱312,600) — they are dominating the high-six-figure to multi-million Hermès transactions; threat to our Hermès revenue share despite our larger inventory.

Miss Manila Luxe sold 83 units MTD on 479 inventory (17.3% STR) — the highest velocity in the market, entirely in entry-to-mid 'Other' brands (avg ₱41,699/unit); not a direct competitor but signals strong mass-accessible luxury demand we are not tapping.

Canone Bags Prime shows 0.0 avg days-to-sell on 1,165 inventory — data anomaly or very recent listings; monitor next 48 hours to determine if they are dumping inventory at ₱33,413 avg ticket which could pressure entry-level LV and Chanel in the market.

Chanel Double Flap Medium: our lowest ask ₱280,000 vs. market low ₱245,900 — we are ₱34,100 (13.9%) above market floor on a 29-listing crowded model; risk of extended sit-time if buyers sort by price.

Shop with K's 61.4% 'Other' brand mix with ₱334,763 avg ticket suggests strong exotic/ultra-luxury non-Hermès sales (Constance appearing in top sold) — opportunity to cross-monitor if Hermès Constance consignments are bypassing us for them.

pricing.suggestion: Our floor at ₱1,288,000 is 35.6% above market low (₱950,000); introduce 1–2 entry condition pieces repriced to ₱1,050,000–₱1,100,000 to compete for budget-conscious buyers while holding exotic/pristine pieces at ₱1,500,000+.
Floor at ₱770,000 vs. market low ₱575,000 (+33.9%) on 16 market listings; reprice at least 1 entry-grade unit to ₱620,000–₱650,000 to appear on price-sorted searches and drive a transaction.

With 28 market listings and our floor at ₱735,000 vs. market low ₱480,000 (+53.1%), our 11-unit position is at serious risk of stagnation — reprice 2–3 lower-grade pieces to ₱510,000–₱550,000 and hold premium HW/exotic pieces above ₱900,000.

23 units at market-matching floor of ₱188,000 in a 35-listing market — differentiate: price Grade A pieces at ₱280,000–₱350,000 with full provenance, mark Grade B to ₱168,000–₱175,000 to clear fast and free capital.

Our ₱280,000 floor is 13.9% above market low ₱245,900 with 29 competing listings; hold if condition/provenance justifies premium, otherwise match to ₱250,000–₱260,000 on 2–3 standard pieces to maintain transaction flow.

Only 4 market listings with our ask at ₱1,540,000 vs. market high ₱1,680,000 — hold or nudge to ₱1,580,000–₱1,620,000; scarcity supports current pricing and there is no reason to discount in this thin market.
